info.name: Authenta-API
info.description:
# Authenta API

Authenta is an AI-powered digital trust platform that helps organizations detect deepfakes, identify manipulated content, verify documents, and analyze the authenticity of digital media.

This Postman collection provides a complete workflow for integrating with Authenta APIs, including job creation, file uploads, processing, and result retrieval.

---

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

Before using the APIs, ensure you have:

- An active Authenta account
    
- A valid Authenta API Key
    
- Postman installed
    

---

## Step 1: Import the Collection

1. Open Postman.
    
2. Click **Import**.
    
3. Select the Authenta Collection JSON file.
    
4. Import the collection.
    

---

## Step 2: Create an Environment

Create a Postman environment to store configuration values.

1. Open **Environments**.
    
2. Click **Create Environment**.
    
3. Name the environment **Authenta**.
    
4. Add the following variables:
    

| Variable | Value |
| --- | --- |
| authenta | [https://platform.authenta.ai](https://platform.authenta.ai/) |
| apikey | YOUR_API_KEY |

1. Save the environment.
    
2. Select the **Authenta** environment from the environment dropdown.
    

---

## Step 3: Configure Authentication

All Authenta APIs require Bearer Token authentication.

Configure the collection authorization as:

**Type**

``` text
Bearer Token

 ```

**Token**

``` text
{{apikey}}

 ```

The collection automatically sends:

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{apikey}}

 ```

No additional authentication setup is required.

---

## Verify Configuration

Before running requests, verify:

- ✓ Authenta environment selected
    
- ✓ `authenta` variable configured
    
- ✓ `apikey` variable configured
    
- ✓ Valid API key available
    

---

# Understanding the Authenta Workflow

Authenta uses an asynchronous processing workflow.

``` text
Create Job
    ↓
Receive Upload URL(s)
    ↓
Upload File(s)
    ↓
Finalize Upload
    ↓
Processing Starts
    ↓
Retrieve Results

 ```

---

# Step 1: Create a Job

Create a new analysis job by specifying:

- The AI model to run
    
- The file metadata
    
- The files that will be uploaded
    

### Example Request

``` json
{
  "inputs": [
    {
      "contentType": "image/jpeg",
      "fileName": "image.jpg",
      "sizeBytes": 414241,
      "slotName": "original"
    }
  ],
  "taskTypeId": "1"
}

 ```

---

## File Upload Requirements

The metadata provided during job creation must exactly match the uploaded file.

| Field | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| fileName | Must exactly match the uploaded file name |
| sizeBytes | Must exactly match the file size in bytes |
| contentType | Must exactly match the file MIME type |
| slotName | Must match the required input slot |

### Example

``` json
{
  "contentType": "image/jpeg",
  "fileName": "image.jpg",
  "sizeBytes": 414241,
  "slotName": "original"
}

 ```

### Important

Any mismatch between the uploaded file and the supplied metadata may result in upload validation failure.

---

## Create Job Response

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "job": {
    "id": "3140",
    "status": "initiated"
  },
  "inputs": [
    {
      "slotName": "original",
      "uploadUrl": "https://...",
      "uploadUrlExpiresAt": "2026-06-14T18:01:53.402Z"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

### Response Fields

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| job.id | Unique identifier for the analysis job |
| job.status | Current job status |
| uploadUrl | Pre-signed URL used to upload the file |
| uploadUrlExpiresAt | Upload URL expiration timestamp |

Store the returned **job.id** as it is required for all future operations.

---

# Step 2: Upload Files

Use the returned `uploadUrl` to upload the file directly to storage.

### Method

``` http
PUT

 ```

### Notes

- Upload the exact file specified during job creation.
    
- Upload URLs expire after a short period.
    
- A successful upload returns HTTP `200 OK`.
    

---

# Step 3: Finalize Upload

After all files have been uploaded successfully, call the Finalize endpoint.

### Example

``` http
POST /api/v1/jobs/{jobId}/finalize

 ```

### Why Finalization Is Required

Finalization informs Authenta that:

- All uploads are complete
    
- Files are ready for processing
    
- Analysis can begin
    

Without finalization:

- Processing will not start
    
- Results will not be generated
    
- The job remains incomplete
    

---

# Step 4: Monitor Processing

Retrieve job status using:

``` http
GET /api/v1/jobs/{jobId}

 ```

### Typical Status Values

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| initiated | Job created |
| processing | Analysis in progress |
| completed | Analysis finished |
| failed | Processing failed |

---

# Step 5: Retrieve Results

Once processing is complete, retrieve the analysis results using the job endpoint.

Results may include:

- Detection outcomes
    
- Confidence scores
    
- Risk indicators
    
- Processing metadata
    
- Generated reports
    
- Model-specific outputs
    

The response structure varies depending on the selected AI model.

---

# Available AI Models

| ID | Model | Description | Supported Formats |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | AI Image Detection | Detects AI-generated images | JPEG, PNG |
| 2 | AI Audio Detection | Detects AI-generated audio | MP3, WAV, M4A |
| 3 | AI Video Detection | Detects AI-generated videos | MP4, MOV |
| 4 | Face Swap Detection | Detects face-swapped videos | MP4, MOV, WebM |
| 5 | Image Forgery Detection | Detects image tampering and manipulation | JPEG, PNG |
| 6 | Document Intelligence | Detects document forgery and tampering | JPEG, PNG, PDF |
| 7 | Face Liveness Detection | Detects presentation attacks and spoofing | JPEG, PNG |
| 8 | Face Intelligence | Face verification and analysis workflow | JPEG, PNG, MP4, MOV, WebM |
| 9 | Face Embedding | Generates face embedding vectors | JPEG, PNG |
| 10 | Audio Splitter | Separates foreground and background audio | MP3, WAV, M4A |
| 11 | EKYC Deepfake Detection | Enhanced deepfake detection for identity verification workflows | MP4, MOV, WebM |

---

# Delete a Job

Delete a job and its associated media.

``` http
DELETE /api/v1/jobs/{jobId}

 ```

---

# Best Practices

- Always use environment variables instead of hardcoded values.
    
- Never expose or share your API key.
    
- Ensure uploaded file metadata exactly matches the actual file.
    
- Finalize uploads after all files are uploaded.
    
- Store the returned `jobId` for future operations.
    
- Large files may require longer processing times.
    
- Monitor job status before requesting results.
    

---

# Support

If you experience issues while integrating with Authenta APIs, verify:

- The Authenta environment is selected.
    
- The API key is valid.
    
- Upload metadata matches the uploaded file.
    
- The upload has been finalized.
    
- Processing has completed before retrieving results.
    

For detailed endpoint specifications, request and response schemas, and integration guidance, refer to the Authenta API Reference documentation.

request.description:
**Description**

> Creates a new analysis task and generates secure upload URL(s) for the media files. Specify the AI model to run along with the file metadata that will be uploaded. 
  

**Notes**

The provided file metadata (`fileName`, `sizeBytes`, and `contentType`) must exactly match the uploaded file.

## Description

> Uploads the media file directly to the secure pre-signed URL returned by the task creation endpoint.

## Notes

- Upload the exact file specified during task creation.
- The upload URL is temporary and intended for a single upload.
- A successful upload returns HTTP `200 OK`.

## Description

> Confirms that all required files have been uploaded successfully and triggers AI processing. 
  

## Notes

- Processing will not begin until the upload is finalized.
    
- Call this endpoint only after all uploads have completed successfully.
    
- The task status will transition to processing after finalization.

## Description

> Retrieves a list of analysis tasks along with their current processing status and metadata. 
  

## Returns

- Job ID
    
- Task Type
    
- Processing Status
    
- Creation Timestamp
    
- Last Updated Timestamp

## Description

> Retrieves detailed information and analysis results for a specific task. 
  

## Returns

- Analysis Results
    
- Confidence Scores
    
- Processing Metadata
    
- Generated Reports
    
- Task Status

## Description

> Permanently removes a task and its associated uploaded media from the platform. 
  

## Notes

- Deleted tasks cannot be recovered.
    
- Associated media and analysis results will also be removed.
